title: Temperature-Controlled DNA Extraction from Ancient Bones
author: Elena Essel
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bje5kjg6

steps.title: Sodium-phosphate buffer (0
steps.instruction: Sodium-phosphate buffer (0.5 M sodium phosphate, pH 7.0, 0.1 % Tween 20) is prepared by combining the following reagents: 49.5 mL Sodium phosphate solution, 0.5 M, pH 7.0 Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #J60158.AP 50 µL TWEEN® 20 Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #T2700-100ML (CAS: 9005-64-5)

steps.title: Tris-Tween wash buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl, pH 8
steps.instruction: Tris-Tween wash buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl, pH 8.0, 0.1% Tween-20) is prepared by combining the following reagents: 49.5 mL Water for HPLC Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #270733 (CAS: 7732-18-5) 0.5 mL Tris buffer pH 8.0 (1 M) for molecular biology AppliChem Catalog #A4577,0500 50 µL TWEEN® 20 Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #T2700-100ML (CAS: 9005-64-5)

steps.title: Lysis buffer (0
steps.instruction: Lysis buffer (0.45 M EDTA, pH 8.0, 0.05% Tween-20 and 0.25 mg/ml proteinase K) is prepared by combining the following reagents: 3.725 mL Water for HPLC Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #270733 (CAS: 7732-18-5) 45 mL EDTA solution pH 8.0 (0.5 M) for molecular biology AppliChem Catalog #A4892,1000 25 µL TWEEN® 20 Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #T2700-100ML (CAS: 9005-64-5) 1.25 mL 10 mg/ml proteinase K solution in water (prepared from Proteinase K 100 mg Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #3115879001 ) Proteinase K is added after UV irradiation

steps.title: All buffers are irradiated with UV-C...
steps.instruction: All buffers are irradiated with UV-C light at a dose of 7 kJ/cm 2 using a cross-linker.

steps.title: In an ancient DNA cleanroom, remove...
steps.instruction: In an ancient DNA cleanroom, remove approximately 50 mg of sample powder from each specimen using a sterile dentist drill and transfer the powder to a 2.0 ml DNA LoBind tube.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.5 mL sodium phosphate buffer to the sample powder, completely resuspend the powder by thorough vortexing, and incubate the tube in a thermo block adjusted to the desired temperature 900 rpm, °C, 15 minutes . Temperature-controlled phosphate treatment steps 37 °C 2 times 60 °C 2 times 90 °C 2 times At least one negative control (tube without sample material) should be included in each experiment and carried through all subsequent steps).

steps.title: To facilitate resuspension of the bone...
steps.instruction: To facilitate resuspension of the bone powder during the subsequent incubation and wash steps, add 3-4 Ceramic beads 2.8 mm VWR International (Avantor) Catalog #432-0292 to the sample material.

steps.title: Transfer tubes to a tabletop centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Transfer tubes to a tabletop centrifuge and spin for 2 min at maximum speed (e.g., 16,400g/13,200 rpm).

steps.title: Transfer supernatant to a 1
steps.instruction: Transfer supernatant to a 1.5 mL LoBind tube and store at -20 °C until the day of DNA extraction. Beads facilitate the resuspension of the sample powder after centrifugation steps, but make it harder to remove supernatant. Pipette slowly and carefully.

steps.title: Repeat steps 7-9 once at each...
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 7-9 once at each temperature (for a total of 2 wash steps). For the 90 °C incubation, make sure the liquid in the tube reaches 90 °C by the end of the 15 min incubation time. If necessary, set the thermo block to a higher temperature.

steps.title: Add 1 mL of lysis buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of lysis buffer to the sample powder, completely resuspended the powder by vortexing, and incubate overnight (8 – 16 h) with rotation at 37 °C Wrap the tube with parafilm to prevent leaking.

steps.title: Thaw the sodium phosphate fractions (and...
steps.instruction: Thaw the sodium phosphate fractions (and lysates if necessary) at 37 °C in a thermo block with gentle shaking. Make sure the liquid is fully thawed and any crystals have completely dissolved. If desired, DNA extraction can also be performed on the Tris-Tween buffer, but DNA yields are expected to be extremely low.

steps.title: Prepare DNA libraries using 20% of...
steps.instruction: Prepare DNA libraries using 20% of the DNA extract as input, following the protocol for library preparation, quantification and indexing by Gansauge et al. (2020). Manual and automated preparation of single-stranded DNA libraries for the sequencing of DNA from ancient biological remains and other sources of highly degraded DNA.. Nature protocols.

steps.title: Trim adapters and merge overlapping paired-end...
steps.instruction: Trim adapters and merge overlapping paired-end reads into single-molecule sequences using leeHom. leeHom: adaptor trimming and merging for Illumina sequencing reads.. Nucleic acids research.

steps.title: The temperature-controlled phosphate treatment is followed...
steps.instruction: The temperature-controlled phosphate treatment is followed by a room-temperature wash step with 1 mL Tris-Tween buffer at the end of the last temperature cycle. Completely resuspend the powder by thorough vortexing.

steps.title: Transfer tubes to a tabletop centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Transfer tubes to a tabletop centrifuge and spin for 2 min at maximum speed (e.g., 16,400g/13,200 rpm)

steps.title: Transfer supernatant to a 1
steps.instruction: Transfer supernatant to a 1.5 mL LoBind tube and store at -20 °C until the day of DNA extraction.

steps.title: Transfer tubes to a tabletop centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Transfer tubes to a tabletop centrifuge and spin for 2 min at maximum speed (commonly at 16,400 g/13,200 rpm).

steps.title: Transfer supernatant to a 1
steps.instruction: Transfer supernatant to a 1.5 mL LoBind tube and proceed to DNA extraction or store the tube at -20 °C until the day of DNA extraction.

steps.title: For the sodium phosphate fractions, purify...
steps.instruction: For the sodium phosphate fractions, purify 100 µl of the supernatant, and for the final lysate, purify 500 µl using binding buffer ‘G’ of the DNA extraction method described in Glocke and Meyer (2017). Final volume of all DNA extracts is 50 µl. Extending the spectrum of DNA sequences retrieved from ancient bones and teeth.. Genome research.

steps.title: Perform shallow shotgun sequencing on Illumina’s...
steps.instruction: Perform shallow shotgun sequencing on Illumina’s MiSeq or HiSeq2500 platforms (or other Illumina platforms) using a paired-end double-index configuration (2x 76 + 2x 7 cycles). Double indexing overcomes inaccuracies in multiplex sequencing on the Illumina platform.. Nucleic acids research.

steps.title: Use the Burrows-Wheeler Aligner (BWA, https://github
steps.instruction: Use the Burrows-Wheeler Aligner (BWA, https://github.com/mpieva/network-aware-bwa) to align merged sequences to a suitable reference genome (e.g. turTru1.75, bosTauUMD3.1, loxAfr4) using ancient parameters (“-n 0.01 –o 2 –l 16500”) allowing more mismatches and indels. Fast and accurate long-read alignment with Burrows-Wheeler transform.. Bioinformatics (Oxford, England). A high-coverage genome sequence from an archaic Denisovan individual.. Science (New York, N.Y.).

steps.title: Restrict further analyses to sequences of...
steps.instruction: Restrict further analyses to sequences of length 35 bp and above to avoid spurious alignments of short sequences with random similarity to the reference genome.

steps.title: Merge sequences with the same start-...
steps.instruction: Merge sequences with the same start- and end-coordinate into one consensus sequence using bam-rmdup ( https://github.com/mpieva/biohazard-tools ).

description: Generate summary statistics using samtools and choose the library with the highest proportion of endogenous DNA for further sequencing. Prepare additional libraries from remaining DNA extract if necessary. The Sequence Alignment/Map format and SAMtools.. Bioinformatics (Oxford, England).
